title: Counterfactual stop-now vs stop-next-lap economics features
summary: Motorsport strategy papers frame pit timing as a dynamic-programming tradeoff between current tyre decay, pit-loss, and future pace.
rationale: Motorsport strategy papers frame pit timing as a dynamic-programming tradeoff between current tyre decay, pit-loss, and future pace. Your best features already encode finish feasibility, but not the immediate economic regret of waiting one more lap versus boxing now.
implementation_hint: Estimate race/year-specific pit-loss from observed PitStop rows and fit coarse compound-specific lap-time decay curves from non-pit laps. For each row, simulate two one-step counterfactuals: stay out one lap then pit, versus pit now onto each feasible fresh compound. Feed the model features like best stop-now advantage, worst delay penalty, and gap between current-compound continuation and best fresh-compound trajectory.
expected_effect: Adds a more decision-like signal than static thresholds, especially around the true stop window where one extra lap becomes expensive.
risk: Can overfit if pit-loss or decay curves are estimated on the full dataset or with target contamination; keep estimates fold-local and simple.
